items.prompt: Which set best matches big-data characteristics covered in this part?
choices: Volume, velocity, variety, veracity, and value
items.explain: Section 9.4.1 presents the Vs including volume, velocity, variety, veracity, and value.

items.prompt: Streaming data must often be processed as it arrives rather than only in periodic batches.
items.explain: Streaming is continuous real-time generation that requires systems for high-throughput flows.

items.prompt: Example 9.24 (noise filtering) primarily addresses which concern?
choices: Irrelevant or noisy social content that can undermine insights
items.explain: Social streams need filtering so analysis is not dominated by irrelevant noise.

items.prompt: Give one example of a streaming data source from Example 9.25.
items.rubric: Accept stock market feeds, IoT sensor readings, or social media updates.
items.sample_answer: Real-time stock market feeds (or IoT sensor readings, or social media updates).

items.prompt: Distributed systems process large datasets by splitting work across multiple machines.
items.explain: Parallel processing across machines supports scale for big and streaming data.
